title: pyrus subvestita
intro: Pyrus subvestita, also known as the subvestite pear, is a species of pear tree native to the Himalayan region. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pyrus subvestita include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Pyrus subvestita include teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
